aws-emr:position-deletes:v2.notes: Supported via Spark on Glue 3.0+ (Iceberg 0.13.1+); Glue 4.0+ (Iceberg 1.0.0) recommended for production

aws-emr:position-deletes:v3.notes: V3 replaces positional deletes with deletion vectors (Puffin files) on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support
EMR 8.0 ships with Iceberg 1.10.1 for full deletion vector support

aws-emr:equality-deletes:v2.notes: Supported via Spark on Glue 3.0+; Glue 4.0+ recommended

aws-emr:equality-deletes:v3.notes: V3 replaces equality deletes with deletion vectors on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support
V3 deletion vectors supersede V2 equality delete files

aws-emr:merge-on-read:v2.notes: Merge-on-read supported via Spark on Glue 3.0+

aws-emr:merge-on-read:v3.notes: Merge-on-read with V3 deletion vectors on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:copy-on-write:v2.notes: Copy-on-write supported via Spark on Glue 3.0+

aws-emr:copy-on-write:v3.notes: Copy-on-write supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Full schema evolution (add, drop, rename, reorder columns) via Spark SQL on Glue 3.0+; ALTER TABLE RENAME not available on Glue 3.0

aws-emr:schema-evolution:v3.notes: Full schema evolution supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:column-default-values:v2.notes: Column default values are a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-emr:column-default-values:v3.notes: Supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0; requires format-version=3
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 with V3 format tables

aws-emr:nanosecond-timestamps:v2.notes: Nanosecond timestamps are a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-emr:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: Supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0; requires format-version=3
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 with V3 format tables

aws-emr:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: Supports identity, bucket, truncate, year, month, day, hour partition transforms via Spark on Glue 3.0+

aws-emr:hidden-partitioning:v3.notes: Full hidden partitioning supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:partition-evolution:v2.notes: Partition evolution supported via Spark SQL ALTER TABLE on Glue 3.0+

aws-emr:partition-evolution:v3.notes: Partition evolution supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:multi-arg-transforms:v2.notes: Multi-argument transforms are a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-emr:multi-arg-transforms:v3.notes: Supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0; requires format-version=3
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 with V3 format tables

aws-emr:time-travel:v2.notes: Query by snapshot ID or timestamp via Spark SQL on Glue 3.0+

aws-emr:time-travel:v3.notes: Time travel supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0; spans V2 and V3 snapshots after upgrade
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:table-maintenance:v2.notes: Spark procedures (rewrite_data_files, expire_snapshots, remove_orphan_files) available on Glue 4.0+; AWS Glue Data Catalog provides automatic compaction, snapshot expiry, and orphan file removal
caveats: Automatic optimization requires AWS Glue Data Catalog configuration

links.label: Working with Iceberg in AWS Glue (prescriptive guidance)

aws-emr:table-maintenance:v3.notes: Full table maintenance for V3 tables on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2; when using AWS Glue Data Catalog, automatic optimization compacts deletion vector files and removes old V2 delete files after upgrade
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support
First compaction after V2→V3 upgrade removes old positional/equality delete files

aws-emr:branching-tagging:v2.notes: Supported via Spark on EMR. Glue: Branching and tagging require Iceberg ≥1.2.0; only available natively on Glue 5.0+ (Iceberg 1.7.1) or via custom JARs on Glue 4.0

aws-emr:branching-tagging:v3.notes: Branching and tagging supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0 with V3 tables
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:read-support:v2.notes: Full read support via Spark on Glue 3.0+; supports GlueContext DynamicFrame and Spark DataFrame APIs

aws-emr:read-support:v3.notes: Full read support for V3 tables on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2; handles deletion vectors transparently
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:write-insert:v2.notes: INSERT INTO, CTAS, and DataFrame append supported via Spark and GlueContext APIs on Glue 3.0+

aws-emr:write-insert:v3.notes: INSERT INTO supported on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 with V3 format tables
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:write-merge-update-delete:v2.notes: MERGE, UPDATE, DELETE supported via Spark SQL with IcebergSparkSessionExtensions on Glue 3.0+; GlueContext native API supports append/overwrite only
caveats: Requires IcebergSparkSessionExtensions for UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE

aws-emr:write-merge-update-delete:v3.notes: MERGE, UPDATE, DELETE supported via Spark SQL on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2; V3 deletion vectors improve write performance for updates and deletes
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support
Requires IcebergSparkSessionExtensions for UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE

aws-emr:catalog-integration:v2.notes: Native AWS Glue Data Catalog integration as Iceberg catalog; also supports Iceberg REST Catalog API

links.label: Working with Iceberg in AWS Glue (prescriptive guidance)

aws-emr:catalog-integration:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 integrates natively with the AWS Glue Data Catalog for V3 tables; the Iceberg REST Catalog API is also supported
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:hive-metastore:v2.notes: Native Hive Metastore support on EMR. Glue: Can connect to external Hive Metastore via custom Spark catalog configuration; Glue Data Catalog is the default and recommended catalog
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration
Not the default catalog; Glue Data Catalog is preferred

aws-emr:hive-metastore:v3.notes: Native Hive Metastore support on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 for V3 tables
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration
Not the default catalog; Glue Data Catalog is preferred

aws-emr:aws-glue-catalog:v2.notes: Native default catalog for AWS Glue; pre-configured as glue_catalog with GlueCatalog implementation

links.label: Working with Iceberg in AWS Glue (prescriptive guidance)

aws-emr:aws-glue-catalog:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 uses the AWS Glue Data Catalog as a default catalog with V3 table support; automatic table optimization available on the catalog side
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:rest-catalog:v2.notes: AWS Glue Data Catalog supports the Iceberg REST Catalog API, enabling cross-engine interoperability

aws-emr:rest-catalog:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 connects to V3 tables via the Iceberg REST Catalog API, including the AWS Glue Data Catalog REST endpoint

aws-emr:nessie:v2.notes: Nessie catalog supported via Spark on EMR. Glue: Can connect to Nessie via custom Spark catalog configuration on Glue; not natively integrated
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration and Nessie endpoint
Nessie JARs must be provided via --extra-jars

aws-emr:nessie:v3.notes: Nessie catalog supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0 with V3 tables
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration, Nessie endpoint, and extra JARs

aws-emr:polaris:v2.notes: Polaris supported via REST Catalog on EMR. Glue: Can connect to Polaris via REST Catalog configuration on Glue
caveats: Requires custom Spark REST Catalog configuration

aws-emr:polaris:v3.notes: Polaris supported via REST Catalog on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 with V3 tables
caveats: Requires custom Spark REST Catalog configuration

aws-emr:unity-catalog:v2.notes: Can connect to Unity Catalog via REST Catalog API on EMR. Glue: AWS Glue does not natively support Databricks Unity Catalog
caveats: Requires Databricks REST Catalog endpoint configuration

aws-emr:unity-catalog:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 can connect to Databricks Unity Catalog via the Iceberg REST Catalog API; no native Unity Catalog integration
caveats: Requires Databricks REST Catalog endpoint configuration

aws-emr:hadoop-catalog:v2.notes: Hadoop catalog supported via Spark on EMR. Glue: Can use Hadoop catalog via custom Spark configuration on Glue; not recommended for production
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration
No atomic rename guarantee on S3; Glue Data Catalog recommended instead

aws-emr:hadoop-catalog:v3.notes: Hadoop catalog supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0 with V3 tables; not recommended for production
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration
No atomic rename guarantee on S3; Glue Data Catalog recommended instead

aws-emr:jdbc-catalog:v2.notes: JDBC catalog supported via Spark on EMR. Glue: Can use JDBC catalog via custom Spark configuration on Glue
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration, JDBC driver, and endpoint

aws-emr:jdbc-catalog:v3.notes: JDBC catalog supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0 with V3 tables
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration, JDBC driver, and endpoint

aws-emr:table-creation:v2.notes: Create Iceberg tables via Spark SQL DDL (CREATE TABLE, CTAS) or Glue Data Catalog CreateTable API on Glue 3.0+
caveats: Glue Data Catalog UpdateTable API does not support Iceberg table format as input

aws-emr:table-creation:v3.notes: Create V3 tables via Spark SQL DDL with format-version=3 on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2; upgrade V2→V3 via ALTER TABLE SET TBLPROPERTIES
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support
V2→V3 upgrade is one-way; cannot downgrade back to V2

aws-emr:variant-type:v2.notes: Variant type is a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-emr:variant-type:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 introduces native VARIANT data type support, enabling JSON and semi-structured data handling on Iceberg V3 tables. (Per AWS EMR 8.0 GA announcement, May 27, 2026.)
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Iceberg 1.10.1)
Variant type support is still maturing in the Iceberg ecosystem

aws-emr:shredded-variant:v2.notes: Shredded variant is a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-emr:shredded-variant:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 supports shredded VARIANT using the standard Apache Parquet shredded implementation, enabling efficient columnar storage and predicate pushdown for nested fields within VARIANT columns.

aws-emr:geometry-type:v2.notes: Geometry type is a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-emr:geometry-type:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 ships with Iceberg 1.10.1, which adds native Geometry and Geography data type support, enabling geospatial workloads on Iceberg V3 tables.

aws-emr:lineage:v2.notes: Row lineage is a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-emr:lineage:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 supports Iceberg V3 row lineage, exposing the `_row_id` and `_last_updated_sequence_number` metadata fields for precise row-level change tracking and audit trails. (Per AWS EMR 8.0 GA announcement, May 27, 2026 — V3 stronger transaction guarantees and data lineage.)
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Iceberg 1.10.1)
No historical backfill after V2→V3 upgrade

aws-emr:statistics:v2.notes: AWS Glue writes column statistics to Iceberg manifest files via Spark on Glue 3.0+. Statistics include min/max values, null counts, and value counts. Configurable via write.metadata.metrics.default and per-column settings.

aws-emr:statistics:v3.notes: Column statistics fully supported via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0 for V3 tables
caveats: Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:bloom-filters:v2.notes: Full bloom filter support via Spark on EMR. Parquet-level bloom filters configurable via write.parquet.bloom-filter-enabled.column.* table properties. Also supports compute_table_stats procedure for Puffin-based statistics.. Glue: Parquet-level bloom filters can be enabled via Iceberg table properties (write.parquet.bloom-filter-enabled.column.*) when writing with Spark on Glue 4.0+. Not enabled by default.
caveats: Requires setting bloom filter table properties per column
Not enabled by default
No Puffin-based bloom filter support

aws-emr:bloom-filters:v3.notes: Full bloom filter support via Spark 4.0.2 on EMR 8.0 with Iceberg V3; Parquet-level bloom filters configurable via Iceberg table properties
caveats: Requires setting bloom filter table properties per column
Requires EMR 8.0 (Spark 4.0.2 / Iceberg 1.10.1) for V3 format support

aws-emr:type-promotion:v2.notes: AWS Glue supports type promotion via Spark SQL ALTER TABLE ALTER COLUMN TYPE on Iceberg tables. Supports int to bigint, float to double, and decimal precision widening.
caveats: Requires Spark SQL on EMR
Metadata-only operation; no data file rewrite required

aws-emr:type-promotion:v3.notes: Type promotion supported via Spark SQL on EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 for V3 Iceberg tables.

aws-emr:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v2.notes: EMR Spark natively supports the Iceberg REST catalog spec via iceberg-spark-runtime. Configure with spark.sql.catalog.<name>.type=rest pointing to the Horizon Catalog endpoint. Direct REST catalog connections support both reads and writes. Note: when routing through AWS Glue Catalog Federation, writes are not supported — that is a Glue/EMR federation path limitation, not an OSS Spark or Horizon Catalog limitation.
caveats: Write via direct REST catalog API is supported

aws-emr:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 ships with iceberg-spark-runtime sufficient for V3 table support against Snowflake Horizon Catalog via the REST Catalog API.
caveats: Requires iceberg-spark-runtime 1.7.0+ for V3 table support

aws-emr:deletion-vectors:v2.notes: Deletion vectors are a V3 feature; not applicable to Iceberg V2

aws-emr:deletion-vectors:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 supports Iceberg V3 deletion vectors (Puffin files), replacing positional delete files for efficient merge-on-read row-level deletes.

links.label: Iceberg v3 deletion vectors on Amazon EMR
